title: Phacelia austrotexana
taxonomy: Phacelia austrotexana is a species in the genus Phacelia, which belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The genus Phacelia is a group of flowering plants commonly known as wild heliotrope or desert heliotrope. This genus is native to the Americas and contains approximately 70 species of plants. The plants in the genus Phacelia are known for their fragrant flowers and are often used as a source of nectar for pollinators. Phacelia austrotexana is a rare species endemic to Texas, adding to its unique classification within the genus.
